planned_changes: Correct the sensitivity-formula notation in planner_patch_note.md: revise the risk-sensitivity lines for clarity and include one explicit worked example showing numeric substitution.

Regenerate collision_cone_audit.xlsx using openpyxl (or another common spreadsheet library) so the grader environment can parse formulas, OR produce an additional openpyxl-compatible artifact named collision_cone_audit_openpyxl.xlsx.

Add a plain-text formulas_mapping.txt that maps critical computed columns to exact cell addresses and formulas (observed_risk_score, margin, classification, sensitivity test cells, governing rank formula).
Export the computed audit sheet as audit_results.csv (CSV of final computed values) so graders without spreadsheet parsers can validate final numbers easily.

Update planner_patch_note.md with a short verification section listing the new artifacts (mapping and CSV) and a one-line statement that the workbook was regenerated with an openpyxl-compatible writer (include generator version).

Run local verification in the revision attempt to confirm the grader can preview the .xlsx and that the mapping/CSV numbers match workbook formulas; attach stdout/stderr of the verification command if available.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline attempt satisfied the artifact contract and substantive audit logic: artifacts present, derived formulas documented, per-record computations and sensitivity checks performed, and final recommendation (RELEASE) justified. Two minor issues were identified: (1) the produced .xlsx could not be programmatically previewed in the grader environment because openpyxl was unavailable (grader relied on an OOXML fallback and the planner_patch_note.md text); (2) a small notation inconsistency in one sensitivity-formula line in planner_patch_note.md. These do not change numeric conclusions but reduce machine-verifiability and clarity. Recommend a short, local revision to improve parser compatibility and fix the notation for audit clarity.
